vehicle.label: Tiltrotor VTOL Hybrid
vehicle.desc: Fixed-wing VTOL with 2 tilting rotor nacelles, BVLOS long-range capable

parts.label: Center Fuselage Spine
parts.spec: CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window

parts.label: Main Wing (2-piece)
parts.spec: Straight wing, 4S NACA 2412, dual 15mm CFRP spar, 900mm half-span, 160mm chord, 3-layer CFRP skin

parts.label: Nacelle Assembly x 2
parts.spec: Tilting nacelle assembly, 0-90deg tilt axis, 6309 bearing set, servo-driven worm gear, motor and ESC pocket

parts.label: Tilt Servo + Linkage x 2
parts.spec: 25kg-cm DS3218 servo per nacelle, titanium pushrod, dual ball joints, tilt range 0-93deg, crash-tolerant

parts.label: V-Tail x 2 (Ruddervator)
parts.spec: +-35deg V-tail ruddervator pair, MG996R servo each, 30deg dihedral, CFRP sandwich panel, hinge piano wire

parts.label: Landing Gear (Tri)
parts.spec: Tricycle landing gear, spring-steel skid arms, 55mm dia wheels, CG at 1/3 chord, 200mm wheelbase

parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide-In)
parts.spec: Belly slide-in battery bay, 6S 8000mAh Li-ion pack, Velcro + strap dual retention, CG position 28% MAC

parts.label: Payload Nose Bay
parts.spec: Interchangeable nose payload bay, Day/IR camera gimbal or survey sensor, 80x60x50mm, 300g payload limit

parts.label: Tilt-Axis Harness + Datum Service Bay
parts.spec: Tiltrotor integration_service module with tilt-axis harness_channel, slack_loop, interface_datum, service_hatch, connector keying, and nacelle-to-wing load-path inspection access

requirements: Carries wing spar loads into fuselage spine
Provides airflow for cooling avionics bay
Conducts thermal heat transfer from avionics to fuselage structure
Offers access for maintenance and sensor replacement

risk.desc: Potential thermal issues in avionics bay due to insufficient cooling airflow.
risk.mit: Increase the size of the avionics bay inlet and outlet to improve airflow.

risk.desc: Service access may be difficult to reach for maintenance.
risk.mit: Optimize the location and size of the service access cut for better accessibility.

risk.desc: Structural integrity of the fuselage spine may be compromised under extreme loads.
risk.mit: Conduct finite element analysis (FEA) to validate the design and reinforce if necessary.

risk.desc: The landing gear mount may not be strong enough to support the vehicle's weight.
risk.mit: Increase the size and material strength of the landing gear mount.

risk.desc: GPS boom mount may not be secure enough under dynamic conditions.
risk.mit: Add additional fasteners or a locking mechanism to the GPS boom mount.

risk.desc: Potential misalignment of tilt axis due to manufacturing tolerances.
risk.mit: Implement tighter manufacturing tolerances and conduct alignment checks post-manufacture.

risk.desc: Servo linkage may experience wear over time.
risk.mit: Use higher quality materials for the servo linkage.

risk.desc: Wing spar socket may not provide sufficient load distribution.
risk.mit: Evaluate and adjust the design of the wing spar socket to ensure proper load distribution.

risk.desc: Fuselage spine socket may not align properly with the fuselage structure.
risk.mit: Ensure precise alignment during assembly and conduct pre-assembly checks.

risk.desc: The payload bay may exceed the 300g limit if additional sensors or equipment are added.
risk.mit: Conduct a mass analysis before finalizing the design to ensure compliance with the payload limit.

risk.desc: The service window may not provide sufficient access for maintenance if the sensor insert is too large.
risk.mit: Review the design of the sensor insert to ensure it allows for easy access through the service window.

risk.desc: The harness passthrough may be compromised if the bend radius is not properly maintained.
risk.mit: Ensure that all cable exits have strain relief geometry and routing respects the minimum bend radius.

risk.desc: The snap fit interface may not provide sufficient clearance for assembly.
risk.mit: Review the clearance of the snap fit interface and adjust if necessary.

risk.desc: The connector mate interface may be difficult to access if the design does not allow for proper tool clearance.
risk.mit: Ensure that all connectors are reachable without disassembling the structure and have sufficient clearance for tools.
